# Figure description: 17.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-VII/17.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **факта фактического материала (map of actual/factual data)** — a data-location or survey-control map at 1:200,000 scale. It is specifically a **point-location map** showing the spatial distribution of all data collection points (boreholes, wells, geophysical stations, or sample points) used in the regional survey. This type of map is standard in Soviet geological reporting to document the density and spatial coverage of actual field measurements.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers **sheet M-42-VII** of the Soviet topographic/geological grid system at **1:200,000 scale**. The map is dated **1984** and was produced by the **Министерство геологии Казахской ССР / Центрально-Казахстанское геолого-производственное объединение** (Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR / Central Kazakhstan Geological Production Association). A stamp indicates it was registered in the **Казахской ССР Республиканский геологический фонд** (Republican Geological Fund of the Kazakh SSR) with inventory number **Инв. № 11572**. The geographic area is in **northern Kazakhstan**. The left (western) half of the map frame is largely empty of data points, while the right (eastern) half contains the bulk of the plotted data.

---

## Key Content

**Spatial distribution of data points:**

- The map is divided by a prominent **vertical boundary** running roughly down the middle, separating a data-sparse western zone from a data-rich eastern zone. This boundary likely represents a survey area boundary or a geological/structural divide.

- **Data points** are shown using several symbol types:
  - **Small circles (○)** — likely boreholes or drill points, many labeled with numbers (e.g., О-597, О-321, О-322, О-278, О-311, О-281, О-284, О-363, О-276, О-275, О-578, О-577, О-68, О-285, О-741, О-324, О-305, О-298, О-719, О-558, О-589, О-321, О-588, О-597, О-507, О-354, etc.)
  - **Larger circles with central dots or filled circles** — possibly seismic shot points or gravity/magnetic stations
  - **Small crosses (+)** or tick marks — possibly geophysical profile measurement points
  - **Numbered points without circles** — possibly surface geological observation points or sample locations, with numbers in the range of 3000–10,000+ (e.g., 7678, 7795, 8277, 8252, 9259, 9255, 9259, 3097, 3060, 4849, 3711, etc.)

- **Dashed lines** — running in roughly NW–SE and NE–SW orientations across the eastern half of the map, forming a **grid pattern**. These likely represent **seismic survey profile lines** or geophysical traverses.

- **Solid irregular lines** — appear in the northern and northeastern portions, possibly representing **geological boundaries, faults, or river/drainage features** traced in the field.

- **Point clustering** is notably dense in the **south-central and southeastern** portions of the mapped area, indicating concentrated drilling or sampling campaigns in those zones.

- **Sparse coverage** in the western half suggests either incomplete survey coverage, a different administrative survey block, or terrain/access limitations.

- No color is used — the map is entirely in **black and white**.
